name: Peking Duck B1
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the duck inside and out, then dry thoroughly.
2. Season the interior with salt.
3. Tie together the ends of 2 green onions and place them in the duck cavity with gingerroot.
4. Bring the edges of the tail opening together and secure with a length of fine wire.
5. Attach another wire to the neck as a handle.
6. Mix water and honey in a large wok or small turkey roasting pan until boiling.
7. Stir in cornstarch dissolved in cold water (1/4 cup). Continue stirring until it reaches a thin stream consistency.
8. Lower heat, holding the duck by the neck wire, dip into the honey mixture 3-4 times to coat all sides.
9. Remove the duck and suspend over a container in a cool place. Set an electric fan towards the duck to help dry the skin. Let the duck drip-dry overnight.
Next day:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
2. Place the duck breast side up on a flat rack in a roasting pan.
3. Roast for 30 minutes.
4. Reduce heat to 300°F (150°C) and turn the duck over. Roast for another 30 minutes.
5. Turn the duck breast side up again and roast for the final 30 minutes.
6. Remove biscuits from the can and divide each biscuit in half.
7. Bring water to a boil in the bottom of a steamer.
8. Place the biscuits in a container above the water, cover, and steam for 5 minutes.
9. Thinly slice the stems of the remaining 6 green onions into diagonal strips (2-inch pieces).
10. Divide the sliced onions between 4 butter plates and place one teaspoon of plum sauce on each plate.
11. Prepare the duck for serving by cutting off the drumsticks and wings, placing the whole duck on a platter.
12. Carefully slice off all skin pieces about 1 inch in size and set aside.
13. Slice meat from bones and place all carved meat on the platter.
14. Cover with skin pieces on the outside to make the presentation look like a whole duck.
15. Eat by making sandwiches of onion, plum sauce, duck, and skin.
